html:
“A tower that shoots a ranged attack at hostile creatures and regenerates 12 Health a second.”

-for Structure

Houndius Shootius is a craftable Item found in the Ancient Pseudoscience Filter. It requires 1 Guardian's Horn, 1 Deerclops Eyeball, and 5 Thulecite to craft, and can only be crafted near an Ancient Pseudoscience Station. Like all items from the Ancient Pseudoscience Filter, Houndius Shootius cannot be prototyped and Characters always need to be near an Ancient Pseudoscience Station to craft them again.

Once placed, the Houndius Shootius becomes a Structure that will act much like an immobile Mob. It will automatically attack Mobs targeting the Character or any Mob that the player attempts to attack, except for Shadow Creatures. The Houndius Shootius briefly emits some light when charging a shot.

It has 1,000 Health and its projectile deals 65 damage per hit. When damaged, it will regenerate 12 Health per second and can also be repaired using non-edible healing Items. Each turret will drain 6.25 per minute when the Character is close to them.

If killed, the Houndius Shootius will drop 1-2 Thulecites and 1-4 Thulecite Fragments. It can also be deconstructed with a Deconstruction Staff for 100% of its ingredients.

Multiple Houndius Shootius can be crafted as the Ruins can be regenerated by killing the Ancient Fuelweaver, allowing 1 additional craft per regeneration.

Fighting Strategy

Below is the number of hits it takes with each weapon to kill a Houndius Shootius when playing with characters with a default damage modifier. The Weather Pain, Brightshade Staff, and Bee Mine are not included due to their random natures. This table does not take into account the damage bonus gained from characters with a lunar or shadow affinity. It also does not take into account the 40% damage bonus Wendy gets from attacking the same mob as Abigail, Wanda's damage bonus with Shadow weapons (except for the Alarming Clock, which does accurately reflect that bonus), or Wolfgang's damage multiplier when he isn't mighty.

It takes 3 blasts with an Ice Staff to freeze Houndius Shootius, and it is immune to being put to sleep.

Note that this table assumes that the Houndius Shootius will not heal, which is impossible. Because of this, it will need a lot more hits than this table shows to kill it.

Skins

Main article: Belongings

Woven - Elegant
Art Nouveau Collection
Intricate Turret
Execute your enemies with exquisite detail.

Tips

  • The Houndius Shootius can be used in conjunction with a Bat Bat to heal quickly; its high Health and passive regeneration makes it ideal to attack and heal (at the cost of Sanity).
  • Despite its name, it can be useful for much more than just Hound attacks.
  • When used alongside the T.I.N.G.L.E. Nodes, most basic mobs will be unable to attack the Houndius Shootius directly, instead running away from the fence.
  • The Houndius Shootius is an excellent item to use to defend the player against Hounds over day 100. However the player could be swarmed before the Houndius Shootius kills the hounds so making 2 will be sufficient enough.
  • Players can use multiple Houndius Shootius to farm different Mobs penned inside Walls. Good examples of this are Bunnymen, and Vargs.
  • Bee Boxes should be placed a good distance away from the Houndius Shootius, as the angry Bees that come out of a harvested Bee Box will be attacked and killed, slowing Honey gathering.
  • Players can use the Hostile Flare to fight Deerclops more than once per Winter season, with the goal to acquire more than one Deerclops Eyeball.

Trivia

  • The Houndius Shootius was first added to Don't Starve in the A Moderately Friendly Update.
  • The Houndius Shootius bears a resemblance to an Obelisk of Light from Command & Conquer.
  • The Houndius Shootius was originally called "Minotaur Clopper". This was changed to "Mino-Clopper", and finally its current name, Houndius Shootius, alluding to its role against Hound attacks.
  • Judging from several character quotes, the Houndius Shootius appears to be sentient.

“I hope it doesn't turn on me.”
–Wilson
“I wish it lit stuff on fire.”
–Willow
“Mighty structure will help me fight!”
–Wolfgang
“I do not know why it helps me.”
–Wendy
“I HAVE TAMED MIGHTY BEASTS TO FIGHT FOR ME.”
–WX-78
“The magic of the two beasts has been concentrated by the Thulecite.”
–Wickerbottom
“That'll drive the hosers off.”
–Woodie
“Some of my better work.”
–Maxwell
“The eye öf the laser göd stares intö my söul.”
–Wigfrid
“Stop looking at me!”
–Webber
“This is my friend, Lazer Oeil!”
–Warly
“Pew Pew rock”
–Wormwood
“That's a fine piece of work.”
–Winona
“Oh me oh my, look at that eye!”
–Wortox
“Glurp, won't hit me... right?”
–Wurt
“It's keeping an eye on me! Get it? Because it's... you know...”
–Walter
“Ooooh, I LIKE this thing!”
–Wanda

Tips[edit | edit source]
The Houndius Shootius can be used in conjunction with a Bat Bat to heal quickly; its high Health and passive regeneration makes it ideal to attack and heal (at the cost of Sanity).
Despite its name, it can be useful for much more than just Hound attacks.
When used alongside the T.I.N.G.L.E. Nodes, most basic mobs will be unable to attack the Houndius Shootius directly, instead running away from the fence.
The Houndius Shootius is an excellent item to use to defend the player against Hounds over day 100. However the player could be swarmed before the Houndius Shootius kills the hounds so making 2 will be sufficient enough.
Players can use multiple Houndius Shootius to farm different Mobs penned inside Walls. Good examples of this are Bunnymen, and Vargs.
Bee Boxes should be placed a good distance away from the Houndius Shootius, as the angry Bees that come out of a harvested Bee Box will be attacked and killed, slowing Honey gathering.
Players can use the Hostile Flare to fight Deerclops more than once per Winter season, with the goal to acquire more than one Deerclops Eyeball.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
The Houndius Shootius was first added to Don't Starve in the A Moderately Friendly Update.
The Houndius Shootius bears a resemblance to an Obelisk of Light from Command & Conquer.
The Houndius Shootius was originally called "Minotaur Clopper". This was changed to "Mino-Clopper", and finally its current name, Houndius Shootius, alluding to its role against Hound attacks.
Judging from several character quotes, the Houndius Shootius appears to be sentient.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Houndius Shootius là một Vật phẩm có thể chế tạo được tìm thấy trong Bộ Lọc Giả Khoa Cổ Đại. Nó cần 1 Sừng Hộ Vệ, 1 Nhãn Cầu Deerclops và 5 Thulecite để chế tạo và chỉ có thể chế tạo gần Trạm Giả Khoa Cổ Đại. Giống như tất cả các vật phẩm từ Bộ Lọc Giả Khoa Cổ Đại, Houndius Shootius không thể tạo nguyên mẫu và các Nhân vật luôn cần ở gần Trạm Giả Khoa Cổ Đại để chế tạo lại chúng.
title: Houndius Shootius/DST
wikitext:
{{Navbutton/Separation}} {{Object Infobox |image = Houndius Shootius Build.png |health = 1000 |damage = 65 |attackPeriod = 3 |attackRange = 15 |mobSanity = -6.25/min |deconstruct = {{Pic24|Guardian's Horn}}x1, {{Pic24|Deerclops Eyeball}}×1, {{Pic24|Thulecite}}×5 |specialAbility = Protects [[Characters]]
Emits light when shooting |spawnCode = "eyeturret" |hauntMob = 50% chance to be frozen }} {{Object Infobox |icon = Houndius Shootius.png |image = Houndius Shootius Dropped.png |name = Houndius Shootius |filter = {{Pic32|Ancient Pseudoscience Filter}} |tier = 6 |crafting = {{Pic24|Guardian's Horn}}x1, {{Pic24|Deerclops Eyeball}}×1, {{Pic24|Thulecite}}×5 |perk = Can be placed as a [[Structures|Structure]] |stack = Does not stack |spawnCode = "eyeturret_item" |description = Keep the nasties away. By killing them. |ingredient1 = Guardian's Horn |multiplier1 = 1 |ingredient2 = Deerclops Eyeball |multiplier2 = 1 |ingredient3 = Thulecite |multiplier3 = 5 }} {{Quotes |wilson = I hope it doesn't turn on me. |willow = I wish it lit stuff on fire. |wolfgang = Mighty structure will help me fight! |wendy = I do not know why it helps me. |wx78 = I HAVE TAMED MIGHTY BEASTS TO FIGHT FOR ME. |wickerbottom = The magic of the two beasts has been concentrated by the Thulecite. |woodie = That'll drive the hosers off. |maxwell = Some of my better work. |wigfrid = The eye öf the laser göd stares intö my söul. |webber = Stop looking at me! |warly = This is my friend, Lazer Oeil! |wormwood = Pew Pew rock |winona = That's a fine piece of work. |wortox = Oh me oh my, look at that eye! |wurt = Glurp, won't hit me... right? |walter = It's keeping an eye on me! Get it? Because it's... you know... |wanda = Ooooh, I LIKE this thing! |choose = wx78}} {{Scrapbook Info|A tower that shoots a ranged attack at hostile creatures and regenerates 12 Health a second.|for Structure}} '''Houndius Shootius''' is a craftable [[Item]] found in the [[Ancient Pseudoscience Filter]]. It requires 1 [[Guardian's Horn]], 1 [[Deerclops Eyeball]], and 5 [[Thulecite]] to craft, and can only be crafted near an [[Ancient Pseudoscience Station]]. Like all items from the Ancient Pseudoscience Filter, Houndius Shootius cannot be prototyped and [[Characters]] always need to be near an Ancient Pseudoscience Station to craft them again. Once placed, the Houndius Shootius becomes a [[Structure]] that will act much like an immobile [[Mob]]. It will automatically attack Mobs targeting the Character or any Mob that the player attempts to attack, except for [[Shadow Creatures]]. The Houndius Shootius briefly emits some light when charging a shot. It has 1,000 [[Health]] and its projectile deals 65 damage per hit. When damaged, it will regenerate 12 Health per second and can also be repaired using non-edible [[:Category:Healing|healing Items]]. Each turret will drain 6.25 {{sanity}} per minute when the Character is close to them. If killed, the Houndius Shootius will drop 1-2 [[Thulecite]]s and 1-4 [[Thulecite Fragments/DST|Thulecite Fragments]]. It can also be deconstructed with a [[Deconstruction Staff/DST|Deconstruction Staff]] for 100% of its ingredients. Multiple Houndius Shootius can be crafted as the Ruins can be regenerated by killing the [[Ancient Fuelweaver]], allowing 1 additional craft per regeneration. == {{Pic|32|Spear}} Fighting Strategy == {{Weapon Hits DST|health=1000|icestaff=3|sleepdart=0}} Note that this table assumes that the Houndius Shootius will not heal, which is impossible. Because of this, it will need a lot more hits than this table shows to kill it. == [[File:Skins Icon.png|32px|link=]] Skins == {{Main|Belongings#Structure Skins|Belongings}}
{{SkinInfo |image = Intricate Turret Icon.png |rarity = Woven - Elegant |collection = Art Nouveau Collection |name = Intricate Turret |description = Execute your enemies with exquisite detail. }}
== [[File:Prototype.png|32px|link=]] Tips == * The Houndius Shootius can be used in conjunction with a [[Bat Bat]] to heal quickly; its high Health and passive regeneration makes it ideal to attack and heal (at the cost of [[Sanity]]). * Despite its name, it can be useful for much more than just Hound attacks. * When used alongside the [[T.I.N.G.L.E. Node|T.I.N.G.L.E. Nodes]], most basic mobs will be unable to attack the Houndius Shootius directly, instead running away from the fence. * The Houndius Shootius is an excellent item to use to defend the player against [[Hounds]] over [[day]] 100. However the player could be swarmed before the Houndius Shootius kills the hounds so making 2 will be sufficient enough. * Players can use multiple Houndius Shootius to farm different Mobs penned inside [[Walls]]. Good examples of this are Bunnymen, and [[Varg]]s. * [[Bee Box]]es should be placed a good distance away from the Houndius Shootius, as the angry [[Bee]]s that come out of a harvested Bee Box will be attacked and killed, slowing [[Honey]] gathering. *Players can use the [[Hostile Flare]] to fight Deerclops more than once per [[Seasons/Winter|Winter]] season, with the goal to acquire more than one Deerclops Eyeball. == [[File:Placeholder.png|32px|link=]] Trivia == * The Houndius Shootius was first added to ''[[Don't Starve]]'' in the ''[[Version History#September 10.2C 2013 - A Moderately Friendly Update|A Moderately Friendly Update]]''. * The Houndius Shootius bears a resemblance to an [[W:c:cnc:Obelisk of Light (Tiberium Wars)|Obelisk of Light]] from ''Command & Conquer''. * The Houndius Shootius was originally called "Minotaur Clopper". This was changed to "Mino-Clopper", and finally its current name, '''Houndius Shootius''', alluding to its role against Hound attacks. * Judging from several character quotes, the Houndius Shootius appears to be sentient. == {{Pic32|Research Notes}} Assets == Houndius Shootius= Houndius Shootius Dropped.png|Dropped Houndius Shootius All Sides.png|All Sides Houndius Shootius All Sides 2.png|All Sides Attack |-| Intricate Turret= Intricate Turret Dropped.png|Dropped Intricate Turret All Sides.png|All Sides Intricate Turret All Sides 2.png|All Sides Attack == {{Pic32|Blueprint}} Gallery == AngryMinoClop.png|A Houndius Shootius about to attack a Spider. turret.jpg|The Houndius Shootius providing light while charging. Eyesnever.png|The Houndius Shootius will stare at the player when they're nearby. Houndius Shootius in Terraria.png|Houndius Shootius in Terraria Houndius Shootius mob in Terraria.png|Houndius Shootius in Terraria {{Craftable Items}} [[fr:Houndius Shootius]] [[Category:Craftable Items]] [[Category:Craftable Structures]] [[Category:Ancient Pseudoscience Filter]] [[Category:Items]] [[Category:Light Sources]] [[Category:Sanity Loss]] [[Category:Structures]] [[Category:Non-Flammable]] __NOTOC__
